C005.claim: Decoherence is a continuous, gradual process caused by environmental interactions, not an abrupt wavefunction collapse.

C007.claim: Decoherence is central to quantum computing, as engineers must mitigate it to maintain qubit coherence.
C007.result_snippet: The utility of decoherence lies in its application to the analysis of probabilities, before and after environmental interaction, and in particular to the vanishing of quantum interference terms after decoherence has occurred. Jul 30, 2024 · The development of quantum information theory and quantum computing has highlighted the impact of decoherence on quantum systems, with researchers working on error correction and fault-tolerant computation to mitigate its effects. Explore what quantum decoherence is, how it affects quantum computing, its causes and consequences, and the leading strategies to mitigate its impact. May 27, 2024 · Explore quantum decoherence: its fundamentals, impact on quantum mechanics, and applications in technology like quantum computing and cryptography. We survey methods for avoiding and mitigating decoherence and give an overview of several experiments that have studied decoherence processes. We also comment on the role decoherence may play in interpretations of quantum mechanics and in addressing foundational questions.
